title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Your Child Deserves the Very Best. Here's How You Can Save For Their Education
Planning for your child’s education is a cornerstone of financial security for families. With the rising cost of education, child education planning has become essential for securing the future of your child’s academic journey. If you’re wondering about higher education savings tips or the best plan for child education, this guide simplifies the process for you.[1]
Why is Child Education Planning Important?
A report by the Ministry of Education highlights that higher education expenses have grown over the past decade. For parents, this means preparing early to build a sufficient corpus for their child’s future needs. Proper child education planning not only helps you stay ahead of inflation, but also ensures that financial constraints do not limit your child’s aspirations. [1]
Benefits of Child Education Plans [2]
Child education plans are tailored financial products designed to address education expenses systematically. These plans provide:
Milestone Payouts: Funds are disbursed at pre-determined intervals, such as the start of higher education. [1]
Life Cover: In case of the parent’s untimely demise, the sum assured ensures the continuity of the child’s education.[1]
Tax Benefits: Premiums paid are eligible for deductions under Section 80C (under old tax regime), and maturity benefits are tax-free under Section 10(10D). [2]
Key Steps to Save for Your Child's Education
1. Estimate Future Education Costs
To start planning, calculate the cost of higher education based on your child’s potential career interests. For instance, tuition fees for engineering or medical studies in India differ from international programs. Consider inflation while estimating future costs. For example: A course costing ₹10 lakhs today may cost approximately ₹24 lakhs in 15 years at an inflation rate of 6%.[1]
2. Set Financial Goals
Break down your financial goals into smaller milestones. This approach helps allocate resources effectively and track progress. Focus on:
Tuition and accommodation costs
Technology and book expenses
Extracurricular requirements
3. Choose the Right Investment Tools
Several investment options are available for child education planning[1]:
Mutual Funds:Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) in equity mutual funds offer higher returns over the long term. These are suitable for parents with a moderate to high risk appetite.
Child Insurance Plans:Insurance-cum-savings plans offer protection and returns, making them a reliable choice for long-term goals.
Public Provident Fund (PPF):With a government-backed interest rate, PPF is a low-risk option suitable for conservative investors.
Fixed Deposits and Bonds:While these offer lower returns, they add stability to your investment portfolio.
Higher Education Savings Tips [3]
1. Start Early
The power of compounding works best when investments are made early. For example, starting to invest ₹5,000 per month in an equity fund offering 12% returns at your child’s birth can grow into ₹25 lakhs by the time they turn 18.
2. Diversify Investments
Allocate your savings across different instruments to minimize risks and maximize returns. Combine equity funds, insurance plans, and fixed-income options for a balanced portfolio.
3. Take Advantage of Tax Benefits
Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, investments like PPF, child education plans, and certain mutual funds qualify for deductions (under old tax regime), helping reduce your taxable income.
Choosing the Best Plan for Child Education
When selecting a savings or insurance plan for education, consider the following factors:
Flexibility:Opt for a plan that allows withdrawals during critical milestones, such as higher secondary school or college admissions.
Insurance Coverage:Child plans often include life coverage for the parent, ensuring financial stability even in unforeseen circumstances.
Returns vs. Risk:Long-term investments typically favour higher returns. Analyse your risk tolerance and select a plan that aligns with your financial goals.
Cost of Premiums:Calculate the affordability of premium payments while considering your monthly budget.
How to Plan for Overseas Education?
For parents planning to send their children abroad for studies, additional considerations include:
Currency fluctuations
Higher living costs
International tax implications
Use foreign currency mutual funds or ETFs to build a corpus in the target currency and mitigate risks associated with exchange rate volatility. [3]
Conclusion
Planning for your child’s education is an essential financial goal. By starting early, diversifying your investments, and leveraging tax benefits, you can secure your child’s future and provide them with the opportunities they deserve. With effective child education planning, your dreams for your child’s academic success can become a reality.[1]
Frequently Asked Questions
Q1: What is the ideal age to start saving for my child’s education?
A: The earlier, the better. Starting at your child’s birth gives your investments more time to grow.
Q2: How do I estimate the future cost of education?
A: Use an education cost calculator to factor in inflation and other related expenses.
Q3: Are child insurance plans mandatory?
A: While not mandatory, they combine life coverage with savings, making them a prudent choice for many families.
Q4: Can education loans supplement savings?
A: Yes, education loans can bridge gaps in funding. However, they involve repayment obligations, so aim to minimize reliance on loans.
Q5: What tax benefits are available for child education planning?
A: Investments in PPF, child plans, and certain mutual funds are eligible for deductions under Section 80C, and education loan interest is deductible under Section 80E. [2]References:
[1] https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/plan/how-to-save-for-your-childs-education/articleshow/111876227.cms?from=mdr [2] https://cleartax.in/s/80c-80-deductions [3] Top 10 Tips to Start Saving For Your Child's Higher Education | Times of India
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Your Child Deserves the Very Best. Here's How You Can Save For Their Education
Planning for your child’s education is a cornerstone of financial security for families. With the rising cost of education, child education planning has become essential for securing the future of your child’s academic journey. If you’re wondering about higher education savings tips or the best plan for child education, this guide simplifies the process for you.
Why is Child Education Planning Important?
Day by day education is becoming a financial strain in India. Schooling costs have risen dramatically in the last decade[1]. But that doesn't mean you should compromise on your child's education planning proper child education planning not only helps you stay ahead of inflation, but also ensures that financial constraints do not limit your child’s aspirations.
Benefits of Child Education Plans
Child education plans are tailored financial products designed to address education expenses systematically. These plans provide:
Milestone Payouts: Funds are disbursed after the lock in period of 5 years, in some case partial withdrawal is also allowed basis insurance company terms and conditions.4 Tax Benefits: Premiums paid are eligible for deductions under Section 80C (under old tax regime), and maturity benefits are tax-free under Section 10(10D).
Key Steps to Save for Your Child's Education
1. Estimate Future Education Costs
To start planning, calculate the cost of higher education based on your child’s potential career interests. For instance, tuition fees for engineering or medical studies in India differ from international programs. Consider inflation while estimating future costs. Besides it is not just the fees of schools colleges that have seen a rise, it is about all the other expenses that come with education. For example, private coaching, music books, school bus, all these expenses can add upto Rs.16-30 lakhs during a child's schooling
2. Set Financial Goals
Break down your financial goals into smaller milestones. This approach helps allocate resources effectively and track progress. Focus on:
● Tuition and accommodation costs ● Technology and book expenses ● Extracurricular requirements
3. Choose the Right Investment Tools
Several investment options are available for child education planning[1]:
● Mutual Funds: Systematic Investment Plans (SIPs) in equity mutual funds offer returns over the long term. These are suitable for parents with a moderate to high risk appetite. ● Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana: If you have a girl child below the age of 10 this is the perfect time to invest in SSY. You can build a corpus for your girl child and avail some tax benefits. ● Public Provident Fund (PPF): With a government-backed interest rate, PPF assures tax free returns. ● Child based Unit Linked Insurance Plans (ULIP) : Insurance cum savings plan offer protection and market-linked returns, making a reliable choice for long term goals.
Higher Education Savings Tips [3]
1. Start Early
The power of compounding works best when investments are made early. For example, starting to invest ₹5,000 per month in an equity fund offering 12% returns at your child’s birth can grow into ₹25 lakhs by the time they turn 18.
2. Diversify Investments
Allocate your savings across different instruments to minimize risks and maximize returns. Combine equity funds, insurance plans, and fixed-income options for a balanced portfolio.
3. Take Advantage of Tax Benefits
Under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, investments like PPF, child education plans, and certain mutual funds qualify for deductions (under old tax regime), helping reduce your taxable income.
Choosing the Best Plan for Child Education[4]
When selecting a savings or insurance plan for education, consider the following factors:
1. Flexibility:
Opt for a plan that allows withdrawals during critical milestones, such as higher secondary school or college admissions. 2. Insurance Coverage:
Child plans often include life coverage for the parent, ensuring financial stability even in unforeseen circumstances. 3. Returns vs. Risk:
Long-term investments typically favour higher returns. Analyse your risk tolerance and select a plan that aligns with your financial goals. 4. Cost of Premiums:
Calculate the affordability of premium payments while considering your monthly budget.
How to Plan for Overseas Education?
For parents planning to send their children abroad for studies, additional considerations include:
● Currency fluctuations ● Higher living costs ● International tax implications
Conclusion
Planning for your child’s education is an essential financial goal. By starting early, diversifying your investments, and leveraging tax benefits, you can secure your child’s future and provide them with the opportunities they deserve. With effective child education planning, your dreams for your child’s academic success can become a reality.
Frequently Asked Questions
Q1: What is the ideal age to start saving for my child’s education?
A: The earlier, the better. Starting at your child’s birth gives your investments more time to grow.
Q2: How do I estimate the future cost of education?
A: Use an education cost calculator to factor in inflation and other related expenses.
Q3: Are child insurance plans mandatory?
A: While not mandatory, they combine life coverage with savings, making them a prudent choice for many families.
Q5: What tax benefits are available for child education planning?
A: Investments in PPF, child plans, and certain mutual funds are eligible for deductions under Section 80C, and higher education loan interest is deductible under Section 80E. [2]
References:
[1] https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/plan/how-to-save-for-your-childs- education/articleshow/111876227.cms?from=mdr
[2] https://cleartax.in/s/80c-80-deductions
[3] https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/education/web-stories/top-10-tips-to-start-saving-for-your- childs-higher-education/photostory/102504139.cms
[4] https://www.etmoney.com/learn/personal-finance/everything-you-need-to-know-about-child- education-plans/ Disclaimer: IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year. ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy. BJAZ-WEB-EC-12377/25
